summary: Matched several previously nonmatching Kirby fighter functions in ftKb_Init.c and tightened one ftKb_Init.h prototype. The PR replaced nonmatching markers with concrete implementations for Kirby motion-file lookup and SpecialN hat-related helpers, including Purin dynamic-bone unload behavior, Koopa/Giga Koopa hat handling, and a small hat-kind predicate used around 0x800F1CD8. The stated goal was 100% matching for four Kirby functions plus a build fix by reverting an unneeded NonMatching and updating the header.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_Init_GetMotionFileString(enum_t arg0), mapping cases 11 and 14 to offsets 14 and 17 and returning ftKb_Init_803CA5A4[offset - 14].

Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F12C8(Fighter_GObj* gobj), retrieving the Fighter with GET_FIGHTER, applying ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C with hat data ft_80459B88.hats[FTKIND_PURIN], then unloading dynamic bones through ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones(fp).
Implemented ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates3(Fighter_GObj* gobj), switching on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind and invoking ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA7D4 for FTKIND_KOOPA and FTKIND_GKOOPS.

Added static helper fn_800F1CA0(HSD_GObj* gobj), reading a u32 at Fighter offset 0x2238 via M2C_FIELD and returning true for values 2, 7, 10, 11, and 13. The excerpt does not show this helper being used in the added code.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8(HSD_GObj* gobj), returning true for Kirby hat kinds FTKIND_CAPTAIN, FTKIND_SEAK, FTKIND_POPO, FTKIND_NANA, and FTKIND_SAMUS, otherwise false.
Updated the header declaration for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8(HSD_GObj* gobj).
smart_moves: Used existing fighter access patterns such as GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and fp->fv.kb.hat.kind instead of introducing new abstractions, keeping the matched code aligned with surrounding ftKirby style.
Converted an unknown header prototype to a concrete return type and argument list only after the C implementation made the function shape evident.
Preserved game-specific fighter-kind names in switch cases, making Kirby hat behavior searchable and easier to audit than raw numeric constants.
Kept the Purin hat cleanup sequence explicit: apply the copied hat data through ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C, then unload dynamic bones with ftCo_UnloadDynamicBones.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby hat-copy code, matching often depends on branching by fp->fv.kb.hat.kind and handling copied-character special cases with FighterKind enum values.
Header cleanup should accompany successful matching: replace UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with concrete signatures when the implementation is known.
Small boolean predicates can match cleanly as switch statements over fighter kinds or state-like integer values, even when the return type in the header is s32.
When matching motion-file lookup helpers, pay close attention to table-index biasing; this PR used offset - 14 against ftKb_Init_803CA5A4 after deriving offset from the input enum.
naming_conventions: Kirby init and SpecialN functions keep the project’s address-suffixed names, such as ftKb_Init_GetMotionFileString, ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates3, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8.
Fighter object parameters use Fighter_GObj* or HSD_GObj* depending on the recovered signature; the header for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8 was updated specifically to HSD_GObj*.
Fighter kind constants are expressed as FTKIND_* enum names, including FTKIND_PURIN, FTKIND_KOOPA, FTKIND_GKOOPS, FTKIND_CAPTAIN, FTKIND_SEAK, FTKIND_POPO, FTKIND_NANA, and FTKIND_SAMUS.
The local Fighter pointer follows the common fp naming convention after GET_FIGHTER(gobj) or gobj->user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used switch-case structures for sparse constants and fighter-kind checks to reproduce branch behavior.
Used M2C_FIELD(fp, u32*, 0x2238) for an as-yet-unnamed Fighter field in fn_800F1CA0, preserving matchability without prematurely naming the struct member.
Returned boolean values through s32-compatible functions, matching the recovered function signature while retaining simple true/false control flow.
Applied table-index biasing in ftKb_Init_GetMotionFileString by computing ftKb_Init_803CA5A4[offset - 14].
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states that the build passed locally and CI was not yet checked at the time of the summary.
The PR body mentions a build fix: reverting an unneeded NonMatching and updating the header.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that name or replace M2C_FIELD(fp, u32*, 0x2238) in Kirby or Fighter structs.
Search for uses of fn_800F1CA0 to determine whether the static helper was connected in a later diff or only included to match this compilation unit.
Look for subsequent ftKirby PRs that further replace UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS declarations in ftKb_Init.h.
Compare other Kirby copied-hat handlers that switch on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind for reusable matching patterns.
